story.asA: a photographer with a collection of Lightroom presets
story.iWant: to convert individual preset files via the command line
story.soThat: I can use them on my Nikon Z camera or integrate conversion into my workflow automation
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Implement Format Detection (AC-2) - Create cmd/cli/format.go with detectFormat() and validateFormat() functions - Case-insensitive extension matching (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate) - Add unit tests in cmd/cli/format_test.go - Run tests: go test ./cmd/cli/
### Task 2: Implement Convert Command Flags (AC-10) - Update cmd/cli/convert.go from Story 3-1 stub - Required flag: --to/-t (target format) - Optional flags: --from/-f, --output/-o, --overwrite - Verify flags with ./recipe convert --help
### Task 3: Implement File I/O and Output Path Logic (AC-3, AC-4) - Create generateOutputPath(), checkOutputExists(), ensureOutputDir() helpers - Default behavior: Replace input extension with target format extension - Overwrite protection: Fail if output exists without --overwrite flag - Add unit tests for path generation
### Task 4: Implement runConvert Function (AC-1, AC-5, AC-6, AC-7, AC-8) - Full conversion logic: Read → Detect → Convert → Write → Display - Single API call to converter.Convert() - Comprehensive error handling with user-friendly messages - Success message with file size and duration - Add formatBytes() and formatDuration() helpers
### Task 5: Integration Testing (All ACs) - Create cmd/cli/convert_integration_test.go - Test basic conversion, file not found, overwrite protection - Use real sample files from testdata/ - Run: go test -v ./cmd/cli/
### Task 6: Manual Testing with Real Files (All ACs) - Test all 6 format combinations (NP3↔XMP, NP3↔lrtemplate, XMP↔lrtemplate) - Test error scenarios: file not found, invalid format, missing --to flag - Test overwrite protection and custom output paths - Document results in Dev Notes
### Task 7: Update Documentation (AC-9) - Update main README.md with CLI usage examples - Add godoc comments to cmd/cli/convert.go - Document supported formats and examples in help text

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-1: Basic Conversion Command** - Command accepts input file and target format: recipe convert INPUT --to FORMAT - Reads input file, calls converter.Convert(), writes output with appropriate extension - Displays success message with filenames, file size, and duration - Exit code 0 on success
**AC-2: Format Auto-Detection** - CLI detects source format from file extension if --from flag omitted - Supported extensions: .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate (case-insensitive) - Error if extension is unrecognized or missing - All three format extensions detected correctly
**AC-3: Custom Output Path** - --output or -o flag specifies custom output path - Default behavior: Replace input extension with target format extension - Output directory created if doesn't exist - Absolute and relative paths supported
**AC-4: Overwrite Protection** - CLI refuses to overwrite existing files by default - --overwrite flag allows overwriting existing files - Clear error message when file exists and --overwrite not specified - Original file preserved when overwrite attempt fails
**AC-5: Error Handling - File Not Found** - Clear error when input file doesn't exist - Error message includes filename - Exit code 1, no output file created
**AC-6: Error Handling - Invalid Format** - Clear error when input file is corrupted or invalid - Error message explains what's wrong - Exit code 1, no output file created
**AC-7: Error Handling - Unsupported Target Format** - Validate target format is one of: np3, xmp, lrtemplate - Clear error for invalid --to value - Exit code 1 or 2 for usage error
**AC-8: Integration with Internal Converter** - CLI imports internal/converter package - Calls converter.Convert(inputBytes, sourceFormat, targetFormat) exactly once - Handles ConversionError type returned from converter - Does not directly call format parsers/generators (architecture constraint)
**AC-9: Success Message Format** - Success message format: ✓ Converted <input> → <output> (<size>, <duration>) - File size displayed in human-readable format (KB, MB) - Duration displayed in milliseconds or seconds - Unicode checkmark (✓) on supported terminals, fallback to "OK" if not
**AC-10: Flag Validation** - --to flag is required (error if omitted) - --from flag is optional (auto-detect by default) - --output flag is optional (default to input path with new extension) - --overwrite flag is optional (default: false) - Invalid flag combinations return usage errors

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
artifact.snippet: CLI-to-Converter API: converter.Convert(input []byte, sourceFormat, targetFormat string) ([]byte, error). New CLI Internal APIs: detectFormat(), convertSingleFile(), formatResult(). Command signatures using Cobra framework with RunE for error handling.

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
artifact.section: Detailed Design - Data Models and Contracts
artifact.snippet: ConversionResult struct with InputFile, OutputFile, SourceFormat, TargetFormat, Success, Error, Duration, FileSize, Warnings fields. ConvertFlags struct with From, To, Output, Verbose, JSON, Overwrite fields.

artifact.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
artifact.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Single File Conversion Flow
artifact.snippet: User Command: recipe convert portrait.xmp --to np3. Flow: Parse CLI flags → Validate args → Detect source format → Read input file → Call converter.Convert() → Write output file → Display result → Exit code 0.

artifact.title: Architecture - Pattern 10: CLI Command Pattern
artifact.snippet: Every CLI command follows Cobra structure with RunE for error handling. Example: var convertCmd = &cobra.Command{Use: "convert [input] [output]", RunE: runConvert}. Flags registered via Flags().StringP(). Error handling via returned error from RunE.

artifact.title: Architecture - Pattern 5: Error Handling
artifact.snippet: Always return wrapped ConversionError for conversion failures. Type ConversionError with Operation, Format, Cause fields. Usage: return &ConversionError{Operation: "parse", Format: from, Cause: err}. Unwrap() method for error chains.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: FR-3.1: Basic CLI command structure with recipe convert INPUT --to FORMAT syntax. FR-3.3: Format auto-detection from file extension. Command-line flags for source/target formats, output paths, and operational modes.

artifact.reason: Main conversion API that CLI will invoke. Thread-safe stateless function: Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error). Supports auto-detection with from="" parameter. Returns ConversionError on failure.
Format auto-detection function used when --from flag omitted. Returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate" based on magic bytes and file structure. CLI will use this in AC-2.
Structured error type that CLI will catch and format for user display. Contains Operation, Format, Cause, and Warnings fields. Implements Error() and Unwrap() methods for error chains.
Format identifier constants ("np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate") that CLI will use for validation and display. Public constants for external use.
Universal intermediate representation used by converter. CLI doesn't interact with this directly, but it's the hub that converter.Convert() uses internally.
Sample XMP file for integration testing. Story references this file in manual testing examples (AC-1, AC-6). Part of 913 XMP sample files in testdata/.
22 sample NP3 files for testing all format combinations. Used in manual testing Task 6 for NP3→XMP and NP3→lrtemplate conversions.
544 sample lrtemplate files for testing. Used in manual testing for lrtemplate→XMP and lrtemplate→NP3 conversions.

package.name: path/filepath
package.usage: Path manipulation (Ext, Dir, Join), glob patterns

package.name: github.com/spf13/cobra
package.usage: CLI framework for command structure, flag parsing, help generation

constraints.constraint: Thin CLI Layer - All business logic must be in internal/converter, CLI only handles I/O and formatting. No direct imports of internal/formats/* packages allowed.
Single API Call - Must call converter.Convert() exactly once per file conversion. No direct calls to format parsers/generators (AC-8).
Stateless Operation - No global state, each convert call is independent. Safe for concurrent execution (future batch mode).
Overwrite Protection - Default behavior must refuse to overwrite existing files without explicit --overwrite flag (AC-4).
Exit Codes - 0 for success, 1 for conversion errors, 2 for usage errors. Consistent with Unix conventions.
User-Friendly Errors - No stack traces in production. Clear actionable messages ("use --overwrite to replace"). Technical details in verbose mode (Story 3-5).
Real Sample Files - Integration tests must use actual files from testdata/ directory (1,501 total files). No mocked file data.
Zero New Dependencies - Use only Go stdlib and existing internal/converter. Cobra already added in Story 3-1.

interface.name: converter.Convert
interface.signature: func Convert(input []byte, from, to string) ([]byte, error)
interface.usage: Main CLI conversion flow. Read file → Call Convert() → Write output. Pass empty string for 'from' to enable auto-detection.

interface.name: converter.DetectFormat
interface.usage: Optional explicit format detection. CLI uses extension-based detection first (AC-2), this function is fallback for content-based detection (Story 3-4).

interface.name: ConversionError
interface.signature: type ConversionError struct { Operation, Format string; Cause error; Warnings []string }
interface.usage: CLI catches this error type to format user-friendly messages. Check Operation field ("parse", "generate", "validate") for context.

interface.name: convertCmd Cobra Command
interface.usage: Command definition from Story 3-1. This story implements the runConvert() function body that was a stub.

tests.standards: Go standard testing package with table-driven tests. Integration tests build CLI binary and execute as subprocess to test real command-line behavior. Use testdata/ sample files for realistic validation. Verify exit codes, stdout/stderr output, and file creation.

locations.location: cmd/cli/format_test.go - Unit tests for format detection

ideas.idea: TestCLI_BasicConversion: Run 'recipe convert testdata/xmp/portrait.xmp --to np3', verify portrait.np3 created, check success message format, validate exit code 0
TestDetectFormat: Table-driven test with .np3, .NP3, .xmp, .lrtemplate extensions. Verify case-insensitive matching. Test unknown extension returns error.
TestGenerateOutputPath: Verify portrait.xmp → portrait.np3 path generation. Test absolute paths, relative paths, files with multiple dots.
TestCLI_OverwriteProtection: Create existing portrait.np3, run convert without --overwrite, verify error and file unchanged. Then run with --overwrite, verify success.
TestCLI_FileNotFound: Run 'recipe convert nonexistent.xmp --to np3', verify error message, exit code 1, no output file created.
TestCLI_InvalidFormat: Create corrupted .xmp file, run convert, verify parser error message is user-friendly, exit code 1.
Code review verification: Grep for 'converter.Convert(' in convert.go, verify single call. Grep for 'internal/formats' imports, should find none.
TestFormatBytes: Verify 1234 → "1.2 KB", 1048576 → "1.0 MB". TestFormatDuration: Verify 15ms and 1.5s formatting.
TestCLI_MissingToFlag: Run 'recipe convert portrait.xmp', verify required flag error, exit code 2.
